Pharaoh would think that all of Israel was entangled in the wilderness, and so would become an easy prey. But God says, I will be honored upon Pharaoh. All people are made to honor their Maker; those who do not honor him, he will honor upon them. What seems to tend to the church's ruin is often overruled to the ruin of the church's enemies. While Pharaoh gratified his malice and revenge, he furthered God’s counsels concerning him. Though with good reason he should have let Israel go, now he was angry with himself for it. God makes the envy and rage of men against his people a torment to themselves. Those who set their faces heavenward and will live godly in Christ Jesus must expect to be attacked by Satan's temptations and terrors. He will not quietly relinquish anyone from his service.

There was no way open to Israel but upward, and that is where their deliverance came from. We may be in the way of duty, following God, and hastening toward heaven, yet we may be troubled on every side. Some cried out to the Lord; their fear led them to pray, and that was well. God brings us into straits so that he may bring us to our knees. Others cried out against Moses; fear set them murmuring as if God were not still able to work miracles. They quarrel with Moses for bringing them out of Egypt; and so they were angry with God for the greatest kindness ever done them; thus gross are the absurdities of unbelief. Moses says, Do not be afraid. It is always our duty and interest, when we cannot get out of troubles, yet to rise above our fears; let them quicken our prayers and endeavors, but not silence our faith and hope. “Stand still,” do not think to save yourselves either by fighting or fleeing; wait God’s orders, and observe them. Compose yourselves, by confidence in God, into peaceful thoughts of the great salvation God is about to work for you. If God brings his people into straits, he will find a way to bring them out.

Moses' silent prayers of faith prevailed more with God than Israel’s loud outcries of fear. The pillar of cloud and fire came behind them, where they needed a guard, and it was a wall between them and their enemies. The word and providence of God have a dark side toward sin and sinners, but a bright and pleasant side toward the people of the Lord. He who divided between light and darkness allotted darkness to the Egyptians and light to the Israelites. Such a difference there will be between the inheritance of the saints in light and that utter darkness which will be the portion of hypocrites forever.

The dividing of the Red Sea was the terror of the Canaanites; the praise and triumph of the Israelites. It was a type of baptism, Israel’s passage through it was typical of the conversion of souls; and the Egyptians being drowned in it was typical of the final ruin of all unrepenting sinners. God showed his almighty power by opening a passage through the waters, some miles over. God can bring his people through the greatest difficulties and force a way where he does not find it. It was an instance of his wonderful favor to his Israel. They went through the sea; they walked upon dry land in the midst of the sea. This was done in order to encourage God’s people in all ages to trust him in the greatest straits. What cannot he do who did this? What will not he do for those that fear and love him, who did this for these murmuring, unbelieving Israelites? Then followed the just and righteous wrath of God upon his and his people's enemies. The ruin of sinners is brought on by their own rage and presumption. They might have let Israel alone, and would not; now they will flee from the face of Israel and cannot. Men will not be convinced until it is too late that those who meddle with God’s people meddle to their own hurt. Moses was ordered to stretch out his hand over the sea; the waters returned and overwhelmed all the host of the Egyptians. Pharaoh and his servants, who had hardened one another in sin, now fell together, not one escaped. The Israelites saw the Egyptians dead upon the sands. The sight very much affected them. While people see God’s works and feel the benefit, they fear him and trust in him. How well were it for us if we were always in as good a frame as sometimes! Behold the end to which a Christian may look forward: his enemies rage and are mighty; but while he holds fast by God, he will pass the waves safely guarded by that very power of his Savior, which shall come down on every spiritual foe. The enemies of his soul whom he has seen today, he will see no more forever.
